name: dev-kit-app
description: The application family kit for dev-factory — binds the dev-kernel contracts to building and validating application code (capability/protocol cells validated by a passing test suite, not a doc check). Exists primarily to PROVE the kernel/kit boundary (TDD §5): adding a second family required ZERO edits to dev-kernel — a different ontology, rubric manifest, validation harness, and dispatch policy, all bound through the same kernel contracts. Stateless; `check-kit-conform` enforces no kernel forking. Also ships the **ui-layout-decomposer** skill — the two-axis UI decomposition method (OUTSIDE-IN macro→micro layout × INSIDE-OUT actions→surfaces) with a gated rubric and an ASCII-wireframe library of four shell archetypes (productivity-shell · saas-dashboard · marketing-site · mobile-app) — because building shippable apps includes laying out their UIs.
